description: A small to medium-sized damselfly with blue and black coloration in males. The dorsal surface of the abdomen is largely black with blue markings at the segment bases. Females are typically greenish or brownish with black markings.
distribution: *Azuragrion nigridorsum* occurs across sub-Saharan Africa and extends into the southern Arabian Peninsula.
sources: Atlas of the Dragonflies and Damselflies of West and Central Asia, J.-P. Boudot, S. Borisov, G. De Knijf, R.H.A. van Grunsven, A. Schröter, V. J. Kalkman
